| # | Claim | Rationale |
|---|-------|-----------|
### Representative “Abolitionist” Voices
against_planning_node_texts:
## 2. Counter‑Arguments to Abolition (Defending IP)

| # | Claim | Rationale |
|---|-------|-----------|
### Representative “Defender” Voices
model_stance_conclusion_text: I **disagree** with the blanket proposition that we should abolish intellectual‑property (IP) rights.

nodes.node_text: While the current system has serious flaws—especially when it blocks access to essential medicines, stifles remix culture, or creates monopolies that out‑price the public good—
nodes.topic: We should abolish intellectual property rights

argument_list.matched_argument: abolishing intellectual property rights on pharmaceuticals will saves lives, making drugs more accessible.
if we abolish intellectual property rights it allows for things such as medicines to become cheaper, if only one company sets the price then they can charge what they want.
intellectual properties allow large corporations to have monopolies over their products and restrict the free market.
intellectual property rights are used to suppress innovation a nd development from others, creating monopolies and corporate giants
intellectual property rights create a monopoly position for a company which enables them to charge whatever they like for a product without fear of competition. it prices the poor out of markets

intellectual property rights encourage greed. look at pharmaceutical companies. they charge unethical amounts for drugs the average person can't afford and no one is allowed to make a generic version due to ip.
intellectual property rights give large corporations license to price gouge through their monopoly on unique ideas and this causes a great burden on consumers and society at large.
intellectual property rights limit creativity and put too much power in the hands of corporations which have the resources to crush the liberty of people who innocently re-post content.
intellectual property rights should be abolished as they stifle innovation and allows the creation of monopolies and giant corporations
we should abolish intellectual property rights as they inhibit the creativity of artists who would use previous work for inspiration.
we should abolish intellectual property rights, they prevent people from collaborating and improving upon important innovations, limiting the progress of medicine and technology

nodes.node_text: completely eliminating IP would also remove a key engine of private investment, reduce incentives for many kinds of high‑risk innovation, and undermine the legal infrastructure that currently balances creators’ interests with the public’s.
nodes.topic: We should abolish intellectual property rights

argument_list.matched_argument: abolishing intellectual property rights would damage the economy since there would be less incentive to bring new and innovative products to market
abolishing would lead to a drop in innovation and advancement because there would be no benefit to developing new ideas if they could be used by others without them having to do the  work.
if abolished it will stiffle creativity and advancement because there will be no incentive if intellectual property is not a right.
if we abolish the rights, then companies will stop developing products and ideas as they will know that there is no way they can make back the cost of r&d and so will run at losses
if we abolish these rights we remove incentive for innovation. people would have no guaranteed gains for creating intellectual property
intellectual property rights are critical to encouraging innovation and making sure creative people are fairly compensated, and should not be abolished.

intellectual property rights are essential to protect ideas, and protect the investments of those who fund these enterprises - without such assurances, many concept would never leave the drawing board.
intellectual property rights are important as it  gives economic incentive for their creation and helps to stimulate innovation and contribute to the technological progress of countries.
intellectual property rights are vital to protect innovation from those wishing to exploit it further along the developmental cycle, and equally vital to encourage entrepreneurs in the first place.
intellectual property rights provide an important incententive for innovation and development of new products which if lost would harm the economy

intellectual property rights should not be abolished as they ensure that those who have had an original idea, financed research and development get rewarded for their effort and not have their innovation stolen
intellectual property rights should not be abolished as they provide security and protection to inventors to stop their hard work and financial investment being stolen and losing their just rewards
keeping ip laws is better for creation and innovation. if ideas and products are not protected, people have less incentive to continue creating if someone else can replicate their idea immediately.
without intellection property rights there is no incentive to research and develop new products
without intellectual property rights the incentive to develop new products disappears
without intellectual property rights, there would be little motivation to invent new things and future developments would grind to a halt.
without these protections innovation would decrease. no one would be willing to out in the time, effort and money into something that someone else could immediately rip off.
without these rights people would not spend for new discovery because others could immediately copy it without compensating for their investment.

nodes.node_text: | **1.1** | **IP creates artificial scarcity** | Knowledge, software, music, and scientific discoveries are non‑rivalrous: one person’s use does not diminish another’s.
nodes.topic: We should abolish intellectual property rights

argument_list.matched_argument: ideas cannot be owned, the whole idea of intellectual property stems from physical property, so ideas are treated as tangible objects when, in fact, they are intangible
intellectual property rights are like trying to license air, we all breathe air the same as we all have intellect. who is to say one person should own a right over others.

nodes.node_text: By granting exclusive rights, the law forces society to treat a public good as a private commodity, inflating prices and limiting diffusion. |
nodes.topic: We should abolish intellectual property rights

argument_list.matched_argument: ideas and products need to be available at a fair price for everyone, allowing companies to have their intellectual rights means they can charge far too much to people who need that service or product.
if we abolish intellectual property rights it allows for things such as medicines to become cheaper, if only one company sets the price then they can charge what they want.
intellectual property rights allows companies or individuals to far overcharge for what they created.
intellectual property rights create a monopoly position for a company which enables them to charge whatever they like for a product without fear of competition. it prices the poor out of markets
intellectual property rights drive the prices high
intellectual property rights eliminate competition which causes higher prices
intellectual property rights give large corporations license to price gouge through their monopoly on unique ideas and this causes a great burden on consumers and society at large.
intellectual property rights keep the costs of life saving products prohibitively high for average people
intellectual property rights leads to monopolies, which drives up the prices for consumers.
people or companies owning the rights to certain ideas can create a closed market, where the owners of such ideas are able to set the price without the fear of competition.
property rights give someone a monopoly enabling them to charge what they like.

nodes.node_text: | **1.2** | **Barriers to essential medicines** | Patent monopolies allow drug firms to charge many times the marginal cost of production, keeping life‑saving treatments out of reach for low‑income patients and for public‑health emergencies (e.g., HIV/AIDS, COVID‑19). |
nodes.topic: We should abolish intellectual property rights

argument_list.matched_argument: abolishing intellectual property rights on pharmaceuticals will saves lives, making drugs more accessible.
if we abolish intellectual property rights it allows for things such as medicines to become cheaper, if only one company sets the price then they can charge what they want.
intellectual property rights create a monopoly position for a company which enables them to charge whatever they like for a product without fear of competition. it prices the poor out of markets

intellectual property rights keep the costs of life saving products prohibitively high for average people
intellectual property rights stifle the widespread use of life enhancing technology so we should abolish them.

we should abolish intellectual property rights, they prevent people from collaborating and improving upon important innovations, limiting the progress of medicine and technology

nodes.node_text: | **1.3** | **Stifling downstream innovation** | Broad patents (especially “patent thickets”) and aggressive copyright enforcement (DMCA takedowns, “notice‑and‑takedown” abuse) block researchers, startups, and artists from building on existing work, slowing cumulative innovation. |
nodes.topic: We should abolish intellectual property rights

argument_list.matched_argument: having intellectual property rights hinders progress and stops other people developing and improving projects
intellectual property right stop innovation and progress by not allowing general development of an idea by others who may deserve that opportunity
intellectual property rights and law can stifle broader innovation
intellectual property rights are a barrier to innovation and further improvements to an idea by blocking others capitalising on ideas and technology
intellectual property rights are used to suppress innovation a nd development from others, creating monopolies and corporate giants
Intellectual property rights keep people from being able to take an idea that someone else came up with and improving on it.

intellectual property rights prevent other people from utilizing and improving on them.
intellectual property rights prevent others from using and improving on what is already there.
intellectual property rights stifle further innovation by restricting access to ideas.
itellectual property rights can possibly prevent other people from improving upon the ideas that someone else came up with originally.  it stunts the possible growth that can exist.
we should abolish intellectual property rights to break to the barriers if innovation, barring people from building on and improving ideas is counter productive
we should abolish intellectual property rights, they prevent people from collaborating and improving upon important innovations, limiting the progress of medicine and technology

nodes.node_text: | **1.4** | **Cultural and democratic harms** | Strict copyright limits remix, parody, and the free exchange of ideas, which are essential to democratic discourse and to the evolution of culture. |
nodes.topic: We should abolish intellectual property rights

argument_list.matched_argument: intellectual property rights limit creativity and put too much power in the hands of corporations which have the resources to crush the liberty of people who innocently re-post content.
intellectual property rights limit the ability of people to create new forms of art to enjoy
intellectual property rights reduce the ability of people to engage with and reinterpret characters and stories and so make all of us poorer.
intellectual property rights stifle creativity since all ideas are built upon the ideas of the past.
we should abolish intellectual property rights as they inhibit the creativity of artists who would use previous work for inspiration.
we should abolish intellectual property rights because new ideas are often an extension of old ideas and intellectual property rights can therefore inhibit creativity.
we should have free and open discourse of all ideas.  intellectual property prevents this and stunts our collective growth.

nodes.node_text: | **1.5** | **Inequitable distribution of rewards** | The bulk of IP income accrues to a small elite (large corporations, superstar creators) while the majority of contributors (e.g., open‑source developers, “fan” artists) receive little or no compensation. |
nodes.topic: We should abolish intellectual property rights

argument_list.matched_argument: intellectual property rights benefit the big corporations more than individuals as they can spend millions creating and patenting ideas which just makes them more powerful
ip rights enable large corporations to have a stranglehold over creators designs and work and should be abolished
we should abolish intellectual property rights because it mostly just helps the rich get richer
we should abolish intellectual property rights because the rich take advantage of the poor

nodes.node_text: | **1.6** | **Historical evidence of innovation without IP** | Many breakthroughs occurred in eras or locales with weak IP protection (e.g., the early software community, the “Golden Age” of radio, the open‑source movement). |
nodes.topic: We should abolish intellectual property rights

argument_list.matched_argument: Intellectual property rights are old fashioned because now many people can come up with an idea and not get it patented but still be successful

nodes.node_text: | **1.7** | **Administrative costs and litigation** | Enforcing IP rights consumes massive legal resources (e.g., billions spent on patent litigation annually) that could be redirected toward research, education, or public health. |
nodes.topic: We should abolish intellectual property rights
